stat_of_week: .655 OBP - James Wood posted a .655 OBP for Athletic Park Dragons this week, an absurd mark that anchored his 8 R / 3 HR / 8 RBI / 2 SB monster line
thriller_note: The only tie of the week came down to General Hospital dominating pitching categories with 70 Ks and an elite 69 IP but getting edged in enough hitting categories by Buttville Skidmarks to split; both teams desperately needed a full win to separate themselves in the standings, leaving them both frustrated.
key_storyline: Athletic Park Dragons delivered the most dominant offensive performance of the week, racking up a league-high 40 runs and a .412 OBP — led by James Wood's video-game .655 OBP and Willson Contreras's 9 RBI — while also winning the pitching battle enough to take down Wisconsin Rapids Reapers 6–3. The Reapers weren't blown out by bad play; they posted a sparkling 1.65 ERA and league-best 1.04 WHIP, but the Dragons' historic offensive output simply overwhelmed them. It's an early statement from Athletic Park that they have the firepower to win ugly or win big.
lucky_reason: Benchwarmers posted the week's worst pitching performance among winners — just 42.1 IP, a 5.53 ERA, and only 34 strikeouts — yet still beat Riverside Roughnecks 6–3, benefiting from the Roughnecks' own pitching implosion and a 0 quality start week to steal categories they had no business winning.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs dominated the pitching categories — posting a 4.30 ERA, 1.13 WHIP, 62 Ks, 5 QS, and a league-leading 7 NSVH — but their anemic .306 OBP and modest hitting surrendered enough offensive categories to the Bombers to lose 5–4, giving them a luck delta of -0.60, the worst in the league this week.
records_broken: Canberra Wombats set a new season record for most home runs by a team in a week with 12, surpassing the previous mark (unknown prior record — 12 HR stands as the new benchmark as of Week 3). Additionally, North Correa tied or set the season record for most stolen bases in a week with 11, matching the current season record.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8–2 week, elite 2.11 ERA, Max Muncy launching HR, deepest all-around roster through 3 weeks | up
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood and Contreras were unstoppable; 40 R and .412 OBP is a league statement | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5-HR explosion anchored a solid 5–3 win; consistent across all three weeks | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Crushed Canberra 8–2 with 64 hits and 9 SB; Bryce Harper's .640 OBP a weapon | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 but posted a 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP — pitching is elite; offense needs to wake up | down
6. North Correa | Lost the matchup but 11 SB and Ohtani's 3 HR show real firepower; speed category dominance is a threat | same
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8–2 but pitching was solid with 75 Ks and 3.99 ERA; hitting depth is the concern | down
8. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is must-start elite, but tying General Hospital when you should've won is a missed opportunity | same
9. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Pitching is genuinely strong — 1.13 WHIP, 7 NSVH — but hitting is a serious structural problem | up
10. General Hospital | Kumar Rocker add is intriguing; 70 Ks in a tie stings, but the pitching foundation is real | down
11. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost 5–4 in a close one; Brice Turang's .474 OBP is a bright spot but pitching remains shaky at 5.05 ERA | same
12. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 4 HR and they still got steamrolled 8–2; pitching is simply not good enough | down
13. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 on the back of Trevor Story's 9 RBI but 0 quality starts and a 5.04 ERA is unsustainable | same
14. Benchwarmers | The 6–3 win flatters a team with a 5.53 ERA and 34 Ks; luck index confirms they should be much lower | same
